sections.paragraph:
ἐκκρεμάννυμι impf. mid. ἐξεκρεμάμην (vernac. form ἐξεκρεμόμην, 3 sg. ἐξεκρέμετο B-D-F §93; W-S. §14, 17; Mlt-H. 206); fem. ptc. ἐκκρεμαμένην (Just., D. 96, 1)

① to suspend someth. out from an area, hang out act. (Jos., Bell. 7, 429) τὶ ἔκ τινος someth. fr. someth. (Aristoph., Eq. 1363) lit. 1 Cl 12:7.

② The mid. (Eur., Thu. et al.; Gen 44:30) is used in a fig. sense to pay close attention to someone or someth., hang on (cp. Philo, Abr. 170 of Abraham’s devotion to Isaac. Ext. of the lit. sense Jos., Bell. 5, 433 of children clinging to morsels) ὁ λαὸς ἐξεκρέματο αὐτοῦ ἀκούων the people hung upon his words Lk 19:48 (cp. Eunap., Vi. Soph. p. 29 τῆς φωνῆς ἐξεκρέμαντο κ. τῶν λόγων); or, the people kept listening to him.—TW.

impf. impf. = imperfect

vernac. vernac. = vernacular

  1. sg. = singular

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

fem. fem. = feminine

ptc. ptc. = participle

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

someth. someth. = something

act. act. = active

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. fr. = from

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Eunap Eunap , IV–V a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 305
